title: Unix Timestamp Converter & Epoch Time Calculator
description: 1.7 billion seconds have passed since the beginning of time! Uncover the hidden milestones of epoch time and decode past, present, and future instantly.

schemas.headline: Unix Timestamp Converter & Epoch Time Calculator
schemas.description: Convert Unix timestamps to dates and vice versa. Live updating epoch time calculator, supporting seconds, milliseconds, and microseconds.

mainEntity.name: What is a unix timestamp converter?

acceptedAnswer.text: A unix timestamp converter is a tool that translates the number of seconds (or milliseconds) since January 1, 1970 (the Unix epoch) into a human-readable date and time, and vice versa.

mainEntity.name: How does an epoch converter work?

acceptedAnswer.text: An epoch converter takes a numeric timestamp representing the Unix epoch and applies timezone offsets and date algorithms to output a formatted string, such as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

mainEntity.name: How to convert timestamp to date?

acceptedAnswer.text: To convert a timestamp to a date, you can use our tool above. Simply paste the Unix timestamp into the 'Timestamp to Date' input field, and it will instantly show the equivalent human-readable date in your local timezone and UTC.

mainEntity.name: What is the current unix timestamp?

acceptedAnswer.text: The current unix timestamp constantly updates every second. It represents the exact number of seconds that have elapsed since midnight on January 1, 1970, UTC.

mainEntity.name: Why do we use epoch time?

acceptedAnswer.text: Epoch time provides a single, simple number to represent a specific point in time, independent of timezones or daylight saving time, making it highly efficient for computers to store, sort, and calculate time differences.
